name: ctx-context
description: Chat participant (@ctx) for persistent project context across AI coding sessions

chatParticipants.name: ctx
chatParticipants.description: Persistent project context for AI coding sessions

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize a new .context/ directory with template files

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show context summary with token estimate

commands.name: agent
commands.description: Print AI-ready context packet

commands.name: drift
commands.description: Detect stale or invalid context

commands.name: recall
commands.description: Browse and search AI session history

commands.name: hook
commands.description: Generate AI tool integration configs (e.g. copilot, claude)

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add a new item to a context file (task, decision, learning)

commands.name: load
commands.description: Output assembled context Markdown

commands.name: compact
commands.description: Archive completed tasks and clean up context

commands.name: sync
commands.description: Reconcile context with codebase

commands.name: complete
commands.description: Mark a task complete by number or description

commands.name: remind
commands.description: Manage session-scoped reminders

commands.name: tasks
commands.description: Task operations: complete, archive, snapshot

commands.name: pad
commands.description: Encrypted scratchpad for sensitive notes

commands.name: notify
commands.description: Send webhook notifications

commands.name: system
commands.description: System diagnostics and bootstrap

commands.name: wrapup
commands.description: Summarize the session for end-of-session wrap-up (read-only)

commands.name: remember
commands.description: Recall project context and present a structured readback

commands.name: next
commands.description: Suggest what to work on next

commands.name: brainstorm
commands.description: Design before implementation: turn a vague idea into a validated design

commands.name: reflect
commands.description: Reflect on session progress at a natural breakpoint

commands.name: spec
commands.description: Scaffold a feature spec from the project template

commands.name: implement
commands.description: Execute a plan step-by-step with verification

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Run verification checks (ctx doctor + drift)

commands.name: map
commands.description: Build and maintain architecture maps (ARCHITECTURE.md)

commands.name: blog
commands.description: Generate a blog post draft from project progress

commands.name: changelog
commands.description: Generate a themed blog post from commits between releases

commands.name: check-links
commands.description: Audit docs for dead links

commands.name: journal
commands.description: Journal management (site, obsidian)

commands.name: consolidate
commands.description: Consolidate redundant entries in LEARNINGS.md or DECISIONS.md

commands.name: audit
commands.description: Run a code-level convention audit across the codebase

commands.name: worktree
commands.description: Manage git worktrees for parallel agent development

commands.name: pause
commands.description: Pause context hooks for current session

commands.name: resume
commands.description: Resume context hooks

commands.name: memory
commands.description: Memory bridge operations (sync, status, diff, import, publish)

commands.name: decisions
commands.description: Manage DECISIONS.md architectural decisions

commands.name: learnings
commands.description: Manage LEARNINGS.md gotchas and lessons

commands.name: config
commands.description: Runtime configuration (switch, status, schema)

commands.name: permissions
commands.description: Permission snapshot and restore

commands.name: changes
commands.description: Show recent codebase changes

commands.name: guide
commands.description: Quick start guide

commands.name: reindex
commands.description: Rebuild context file indices

commands.name: why
commands.description: Show rationale for context design decisions

disambiguation.description: The user wants to manage persistent project context, initialize a .context/ directory, check context status, detect drift, recall session history, or load context for AI sessions.
examples: Add a task to my context

ctx.executablePath.description: Path to the ctx executable. Defaults to 'ctx' (found via PATH).
